title: Use Claude Agent SDK instead of subprocess spawning
source: Claude Agent SDK documentation and multi-agent orchestration best practices (Anthropic docs, 2025-2026)
description: The system currently spawns Morty agents via `subprocess.run(['claude', '-p', ...])` which is brittle, loses structured output, and has no native support for streaming, tool use, or conversation continuity. The Claude Agent SDK (claude_agent_sdk) provides a Python-native way to orchestrate sub-agents with proper typed outputs, tool definitions, streaming callbacks, and conversation management — eliminating shell escaping issues and enabling richer agent-to-agent communication.
proposed_changes: Replace `delegate_to_agent()` in delegate.py (line 778) to use the Claude Agent SDK's `Agent` class instead of subprocess. Create a new `scripts/agent_client.py` module that wraps the SDK: (1) Initialize an Agent with model, system prompt from agent JSON configs, and MCP tools; (2) Use `agent.run()` with structured output schemas for ticket results; (3) Add streaming callbacks that emit roro events in real-time instead of waiting for full completion; (4) Update orchestrate.py's sprint loop to use agent handles for concurrent execution via asyncio instead of `concurrent.futures` with subprocess. Keep `claude -p` as a fallback via env var `CTO_USE_SDK=true` to enable gradual migration.
